mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer spending strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum and rising factory utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias (win rate < 52%) during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a multivariate regression controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity, which immediately improves corporate earnings revisions and triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >53% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening business investment, which reduces near-term profit expectations and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of contraction months, or the signal fails to predict returns after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
